subject: [SO] Add Camunda BPM Properties to SO-NFVO-LCM
commit_message:
[SO] Add Camunda BPM Properties to SO-NFVO-LCM

Additional properties needed for so-etsi-nfvo-ns-lcm

Issue-ID: SO-3258
Change-Id: I7d88acf5f20b7eeee90435c61bd15f9b616761bd
Signed-off-by: AndrewLamb <andrew.a.lamb@est.tech>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+2

just have a question before merge.
I see in logs:
2020-10-21T11:32:36.553Z||main|||||ERROR|500||Error reading truststore, TLS connection will fail.
java.io.FileNotFoundException: ServletContext resource [/opt/app/osaaf/local/org.onap.so.trust.jks] cannot be resolved to URL because it does not exist

and /opt/app/osaaf/local/org.onap.so.trust.jks is the place where the truststore is supposed to be (at least that's what we see in cert initializer logs)

any reason for that?
Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1
Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review+2
> 
> just have a question before merge.
> I see in logs:
> 2020-10-21T11:32:36.553Z||main|||||ERROR|500||Error reading truststore, TLS connection will fail.
> java.io.FileNotFoundException: ServletContext resource [/opt/app/osaaf/local/org.onap.so.trust.jks] cannot be resolved to URL because it does not exist
> 
> and /opt/app/osaaf/local/org.onap.so.trust.jks is the place where the truststore is supposed to be (at least that's what we see in cert initializer logs)
> 
> any reason for that?

What pod are you seeing this??? if it is spring boot application and you have property defined for it in application.yaml then try it with "file:/opt/app/osaaf/local/org.onap.so.trust.jks" and make sure you have correct permission for this file

well, the more I see etsi-nfvo, the less I understand :( 

in override.yaml, I can see ${TRUSTSTORE}

so I believe from what you say that the value of ${TRUSTSTORE} should be file:/opt/app/osaaf/local/org.onap.so.trust.jks no?

But, I don't see where this value is set ($TRUSTORE) as it's not in the deployment ENV part...

for the msb part, it should be there, although MSB should move to AAF but that's another story, but I've just seen that the import init container didn't worked so I have to dig into that.

ok, my bad
truststore is coming from "{{ include "so.certificates.env" . | indent 12 | trim }}"


can I set this in override file:
        trust-store: file:/${TRUSTSTORE}

?
or do I need to add the "file:/" into the env variable? (I'd prefer not too as it may have consequences on other pods)

Change it to following should work 

trust-store: file:${TRUSTSTORE}
